I'll help you find all unresolved Sentry issues in the 'billing' project. Let me first explore the sentry_issues table structure, then query for the unresolved ones.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'sentry_issues' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

text: Now I can see the table has a `project` column and a `status` column. I'll query for unresolved Sentry issues in the 'billing' project:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, level, count, status, project FROM sentry_issues WHERE project = 'billing' AND status = 'unresolved' ORDER BY id"}

data: {"rows":[{"id":"SENTRY-3002","title":"5xx: CSV export truncates large accounts","level":"error","count":838,"status":"unresolved","project":"billing"},{"id":"SENTRY-3015","title":"OOMKilled: SSO SCIM provisioning errors","level":"warning","count":524,"status":"unresolved","project":"billing"},{"id":…(+919)

text: Now I'll count them to get the exact number:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as unresolved_count FROM sentry_issues WHERE project = 'billing' AND status = 'unresolved'"}
